methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Mara is a seed-stage company in the Defense & Security sector. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Stark Defence ($500M Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M Series A), Ares Industries ($150M Series A), and Tenna Systems ($60M seed). This cohort reflects both public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and private frontier-tech players active in the sector.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach given available data. The median valuation across the four comparables is $225M, with a range spanning $60M to $500M. This method yields an implied valuation of $225M for Mara. The wider comp universe includes eight private peers and two public references, though the core analysis relies on the tighter four-company set to isolate stage and sector alignment.

The triangulated valuation range supports $191M (low), $225M (median), and $259M (high). The relatively wide dispersion within the peer set—driven by stage heterogeneity and company-specific risk profiles—creates meaningful uncertainty around this midpoint. Key sensitivities include Mara's differentiation within Defense & Security, actual product maturity relative to comparables, and customer traction relative to seed-stage peers like Tenna Systems versus Series A entrants.
